_meta.description: Persistent living knowledge for the Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor (SCEM). Carries forward conflict baselines (indicator medians build over 13 weeks), F-flag history, and roster status across weekly issues. Extended by DR baseliner 2026-04-28 with spec-mandated structural modules.

baseliner_corrections.reason: Subagent emitted last_issue=0 (first-build pattern); current state has last_issue=8. Operator-side correction during consolidation.

conflict_pair_registry.status: Active — elevated; Eastern Sentry launched Sep 2025
Active — fragile ceasefire (Trump Oct-2025 framework); persistent violations; Hamas rejected disarmament 14 Apr 2026

conflict_pair_registry.display_name: US–Israel vs Iran (Op Epic Fury / Op Roaring Lion)
conflict_pair_registry.status: Active — war on; ceasefire fragile; Hormuz blockade by both sides; nuclear talks at impasse Apr 2026
Active — ceasefire brokered Apr 16 2026 now unraveling; IDF ground forces remain; new evacuation orders Apr 27
Active — sustained PLA grey-zone pressure; Justice Mission 2025 Dec 2026; US-PH Balikatan 2026 ongoing Apr
Active — DPRK troops deployed Russia; 7th missile test 2026 Apr; Kim ordered nuclear acceleration Feb 2026
Latent escalatory — post-Op Sindoor May 2025 ceasefire; residual tension; CFR rates 'moderate' conflict risk 2026
Managed tension — post-Galwan partial disengagement holds; structural competition in Indian Ocean continues
Active — year 4; Kordofan new flashpoint; RSF captured El Fasher Oct 2025; 14M displaced
Active — year 5+; junta controls ~21% territory; SCEF resistance alliance formed Apr 2026; Min Aung Hlaing sworn as president Apr 10 2026
Active — ceasefire fragile; Switzerland talks Apr 16-19 2026 produced humanitarian protocol; South Kivu fighting continues
Active — MSS transitioning to GSF (Haitian-led security force); gang control of ~85% Port-au-Prince

order_of_battle.deployed_positions: Eastern Ukraine front — Lyman, Kupyansk, Pokrovsk, Chasiv Yar, Donetsk axes; Kherson direction; Zaporizhzhia steppe

key_capabilities.precision_strike: Iskander-M ballistic, Kh-101/Kh-59/Kh-69 cruise missiles, Kinzhal (limited)
key_capabilities.drones: Shahed-136/Gerbera/Italmas (Iran-sourced); Lancet loitering munition; Molniya FPV — mass-employment doctrine
key_capabilities.armour: T-72/T-80/T-90; significant attrition — replaced by older stocks and DPRK-supplied munitions

order_of_battle.notable: Spring-Summer 2026 offensive begun ~Mar 17; largest single strike package in war history launched Mar 23-24 (~1000 projectiles); Ukraine drone advantage degrading Russian advance tempo
order_of_battle.dprk_contingent: ~11,000 troops in Kursk Oblast; 6,000 casualties to date; regular rotations; artillery units active since Jan 2026

order_of_battle.deployed_positions: Sumy/Kharkiv sector; Fortress Belt (Lyman-Slovyansk-Kramatorsk); Pokrovsk axis; Zaporizhzhia-Dnipropetrovsk front

key_capabilities.drones: 11,000+ combat UAV missions per day (Syrskyi Apr 9 2026); 41 mid-range strikes Jan → 115 Mar 2026; drone advantage confirmed vs Russia
key_capabilities.air_defence: US Patriot (critical shortage); NASAMS; European systems; degraded by Russian strikes on energy
key_capabilities.strike: Mid-range strikes against Russian logistics/command posts in near-rear; strikes on Russian oil infrastructure (Tuapse Apr 28)

order_of_battle.notable: Most significant territorial gains since Kursk incursion Aug 2024; recently liberated Andriivka (Sumy north) Apr 21; Ukraine preparations for summer counter-offensive

order_of_battle.deployed_positions: Gaza (Yellow Line perimeter); Southern Lebanon (Litani buffer zone, ground forces); Persian Gulf / Red Sea (joint with US)

key_capabilities.air: F-35I Adir; F-15I Ra'am; multi-axis precision strike; 700+ Lebanon sorties documented Mar 2026
key_capabilities.ground: 98th Division + second division in Lebanon; Golani in Gaza perimeter

order_of_battle.notable: Operation Eternal Darkness (Apr 8 2026) — 357 killed, 100 airstrikes; IDF ground invasion Lebanon since Mar 16 2026

order_of_battle.deployed_positions: Persian Gulf / Strait of Hormuz; Hormuz closure enforced

key_capabilities.nuclear_programme: ~400kg 60%-enriched uranium at unknown location post-US/Israeli strikes; centrifuge capacity degraded
key_capabilities.missiles: Ballistic missile program — reduced after Israeli strikes on arsenal; Rubio insists must be included in talks
key_capabilities.proxies: Houthis/Ansar Allah (Red Sea); remaining Hezbollah capacity; Iraq-based militias — substantially degraded by Israeli strikes

order_of_battle.notable: US-Israeli strikes June 2025 destroyed key nuclear sites + air defences; Hormuz running ~6 vessels/day vs 135 normal; Iran proposed Apr 27 to reopen Strait separate from nuclear talks — US described offer as 'not good enough'

order_of_battle.standing_strength: Active PLAN Task Group 107 — live-fire drills east of Philippines Apr 25-26 in response to Balikatan 2026

key_capabilities.exercises: Justice Mission 2025 (Dec 29-30 2025) — largest Taiwan blockade simulation since 2022; zones entered Taiwan's territorial waters; 27 rockets near Keelung
key_capabilities.aviation: 274 ADIZ sorties Dec 2025; sustained higher operational tempo post-Lai inauguration
key_capabilities.naval: Type 075 amphibious assault ship 'Hainan' first deployed in major Taiwan exercise

order_of_battle.notable: Two large-scale exercises 2025 (Strait Thunder-2025A Apr; Justice Mission 2025 Dec); targeting Taiwanese energy facilities publicly stated; no carrier deployed in Dec exercise — assessed blockade/decapitation focus

order_of_battle.standing_strength: ~11,000 troops in Russia Kursk (early 2026); 11th 'Storm' Corps special forces + artillery

key_capabilities.nuclear: Est. ~50 warheads; Yongbyon complex expansion underway; Kim ordered acceleration Feb 2026
key_capabilities.missiles: 7th launch 2026 (Apr 21) — Hwasong-11 cluster-bomb warhead variants; SLBM program active
key_capabilities.artillery: MLRS + howitzers active in Kursk vs Ukraine border targets

order_of_battle.notable: DPRK seeking US recognition as nuclear-armed state (Arms Control Apr 2026); US redeploying Korean assets to Middle East per reports

treaty_posture.details: Expired Feb 5 2026. Russia declared obligations 'no longer binding' Feb 4 2026. Informal handshake deal negotiated in Abu Dhabi to observe central limits for ~6 months while new treaty discussed — not formally signed, requires presidential approval. No legally binding successor agreement.

US withdrew 2019; Russia suspended 2023. No successor. Both parties now free to develop and deploy INF-range ground-launched missiles. Russia has resumed testing; US developing non-nuclear INF-range systems.

In force. DPRK: withdrew 2003 — conducting active nuclear weapons development. Iran: NPT member, JCPOA collapsed, enrichment ceased post-US strikes June 2025, ~400kg 60%-enriched uranium at unknown location. India/Pakistan: not NPT parties. Israel: not NPT party (ambiguous posture).
treaty_posture.iran_compliance: Non-compliant — enrichment exceeded limits pre-strikes; post-strikes compliance status unclear given unknown stockpile location
treaty_posture.dprk_compliance: Non-compliant — active ICBM + nuclear warhead program

treaty_posture.details: US withdrew 2020; Russia withdrew 2021. Effectively defunct.

Has not entered into force — US, China, India, Pakistan, Israel, Egypt, Iran not ratified. DPRK conducted nuclear tests through 2017; no test detected since. Russia ratified. No confirmed tests by any state 2024-2026 (monitoring period).

US withdrew 2018 (Trump 1st term); Iran progressively exceeded enrichment limits; talks to restore collapsed. US/Israeli strikes June 2025 destroyed major centrifuge capacity. Current US-Iran talks (Oman/Pakistan mediation) focused on post-war settlement, not JCPOA restoration. US demands Iran never obtain nuclear weapon; Iran demands nuclear program not on table until Hormuz reopened.
Minsk I (2014) and Minsk II (2015) — both effectively defunct since Feb 2022 full-scale invasion.
Signed June 2024 during Putin visit to Pyongyang. Mutual defence clause invoked to justify DPRK troop deployment. Includes munitions supply and military technology transfer provisions.

stated_red_lines: Direct NATO military involvement in combat operations on Ukrainian territory
Strikes on Russian territory with Western-supplied long-range weapons (declared red line, partially crossed — Kremlin recalibrated response)

demonstrated_red_lines_crossed.event: Ukraine ATACMS strikes on Crimea and Russian-held territory
demonstrated_red_lines_crossed.response: Intensified strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure; no nuclear escalation; threshold crossed without nuclear response
demonstrated_red_lines_crossed.significance: Demonstrates red line was not a hard stop

demonstrated_red_lines_crossed.response: Counter-offensive with DPRK troops; no escalation to nuclear; conventional response
demonstrated_red_lines_crossed.significance: Major territorial crossing absorbed without strategic escalation

nuclear_doctrine.doctrine_note: Russia formally lowered threshold for nuclear use in response to conventional attacks supported by nuclear states. PERSISTENT BASELINE SHIFT. Subsequent Medvedev/MFA rhetoric is episodic. First use now explicitly permitted if Russian state existence threatened OR if conventional attack supported by nuclear-armed ally.
nuclear_doctrine.current_level: Elevated signalling; no operational deployment change confirmed

stated_red_lines: Iran obtaining a nuclear weapon — Secretary Rubio: 'definitively prevents them from sprinting towards a nuclear weapon at any point'
Hormuz closure normalized — Rubio: 'international waterways, cannot tolerate them trying to normalize a system in which Iranians decide who gets to use'

demonstrated_red_lines_crossed.response: Continued blockade; nuclear demand remains central to US negotiating position
demonstrated_red_lines_crossed.significance: US does not consider nuclear risk resolved

nuclear_doctrine.posture: Extended deterrence — US does not permit Iran nuclear acquisition
nuclear_doctrine.current_status: War ongoing; Iran nuclear program substantially degraded but not eliminated

stated_red_lines: Nuclear program non-negotiable until Hormuz reopened and blockade lifted
Ballistic missile program off the table per Araghchi statements

nuclear_doctrine.posture: Ambiguous — no official nuclear weapons doctrine; ~400kg 60%-enriched uranium at unknown location; Shamkhani: ending program or exporting uranium 'nonstarters'
nuclear_doctrine.current_status: Centrifuge capacity substantially degraded by US/Israeli strikes June 2025; enrichment halted (forced); stockpile location unknown

stated_red_lines: Any US/ROK first strike on DPRK nuclear facilities
US refusal to acknowledge DPRK nuclear status — Kim seeking nuclear recognition as precondition for any talks

nuclear_doctrine.posture: First use doctrine — DPRK 2022 law permits nuclear first use if regime existence threatened; Kim ordered nuclear acceleration Feb 2026; ~50 estimated warheads
nuclear_doctrine.current_status: HIGH. 7 launches in 2026 by Apr 21; Hwasong-11 cluster-bomb warhead variants; Yongbyon expansion; SLBM active

stated_red_lines: US arms sales above tolerance threshold (recent $11.1bn package triggered Justice Mission 2025 Dec drills)

nuclear_doctrine.posture: No-first-use declared — BUT ongoing expansion to 1,000+ warhead capability by 2035 (DoD 2025 assessment); lesson-drawing from Iran-Israel conflict for I3 signalling
nuclear_doctrine.current_status: No imminent first-use signals; PLA grey-zone operations maintained at elevated tempo

stated_red_lines: High-casualty terrorist attack traceable to Pakistan state — Modi doctrine post-Op Sindoor: 'future attacks met with swift and forceful retaliation'
Pakistan nuclear first-use threat — Modi explicitly rejected as deterrent: 'such threats will not deter India from targeting terrorist infrastructure'

nuclear_doctrine.posture: No-first-use declared; credible minimum deterrence; strategic forces triad (Agni-series, sub-launched Sagarika, aircraft)
nuclear_doctrine.current_status: Stable; post-Op Sindoor India demonstrated willingness to strike Pakistan across LoC without nuclear escalation

stated_red_lines: Indian conventional forces crossing international border in strength (tripwire to nuclear signalling)

nuclear_doctrine.posture: First use permitted — lower threshold than India; full-spectrum deterrence includes tactical nuclear weapons (Nasr/Hatf-9); Army Chief Munir reaffirmed two-nation theory 2025
nuclear_doctrine.current_status: Elevated signalling after Op Sindoor; nuclear umbrella explicitly invoked but India did not desist

active_escalation_events.event: Russia launches Spring-Summer 2026 offensive — ISW assessment: offensive began ~Mar 17-21 with marked increase in mechanised/motorised assaults across multiple sectors
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Major escalation step — sustained offensive with tens of thousands of casualties in first week; largest single strike package in war history (Mar 23-24: ~1000 drones/missiles)

active_escalation_events.event: Orthodox Easter ceasefire — 32-hour truce agreed by both sides; ceasefire collapses amid mutual accusations of thousands of violations; Russia rejects Zelensky proposal for trilateral leaders' meeting
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Diplomatic failure; confirms no peace pathway near-term; Russia 'not top priority' (Lavrov Apr 18)

active_escalation_events.event: New START expires — no successor; informal US-Russia handshake to observe limits for 6 months; no formal agreement
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: First time since 1972 no legally binding nuclear arms control agreement between US and Russia

active_escalation_events.event: ~11,000 DPRK troops confirmed in Russia's Kursk Oblast (South Korean NIS); 6,000 casualties; regular rotations under DPRK-Russia Comprehensive Strategic Partnership; North Korean artillery units active Jan 2026
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Third-party nuclear-armed state troops directly participating in European war — unprecedented since Korea 1950

active_escalation_events.event: US-Israel launches strikes on Iran (Operation Epic Fury / Roaring Lion) — onset of direct US-Iran/Israel kinetic conflict; Strait of Hormuz blockade effective; CENTCOM claims capabilities 'dismantled'
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Largest regional war in Middle East since 1973; Hormuz closure largest oil disruption in history (IEA); Brent >$120

active_escalation_events.event: Indirect US-Iran nuclear talks in Muscat (Oman mediation) — 'consultations focused on preparing appropriate circumstances for resuming diplomatic and technical negotiations'; no agreement
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: First diplomatic contact since war onset; Iranian leadership divided on nuclear concessions; talks described as 'totally an impasse' by Iranian official Apr 27

active_escalation_events.event: Iran proposes decoupling Hormuz from nuclear talks — offer to reopen Strait + end war if US lifts blockade; nuclear talks deferred to later stage; conveyed via Pakistan intermediaries; US described it as 'not good enough'
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Signals Iranian leadership split; US leverage via blockade; impasse on nuclear — no off-ramp visible

active_escalation_events.event: IDF ground invasion of Lebanon begins — first ground invasion since 2006; second division deployed; Litani buffer zone operations; UNIFIL mandate effectively broken; 1M+ displaced (20% of Lebanon)
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Major escalation — ground war in Lebanon while Gaza ceasefire nominally holds

active_escalation_events.event: Operation Eternal Darkness — Israeli strikes on Lebanon kill 357, wound hundreds; condemned by 20+ states and UN; Black Wednesday; disrupted Iran war ceasefire talks
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Highest single-day casualty event in Lebanon war; ceasefire negotiations triggered

active_escalation_events.event: US-brokered ceasefire (3-week) begins; Trump extends to mid-May; ceasefire already unraveling by Apr 26 — Netanyahu orders IDF to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah; Israel issues new evacuation orders for 7 towns Apr 27
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Pattern: ceasefires nominal only; IDF maintains kinetic operations; Hezbollah drones targeting Israeli helicopter Apr 26

active_escalation_events.event: Hamas rejects disarmament clause of Gaza peace plan — refused Board of Peace phased disarmament plan presented by HR Mladenov; IDF reports 14 ceasefire violations Apr 8-16; death toll since ceasefire Oct 2025 exceeds 984
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Core impasse for 2nd phase of Comprehensive Plan; disarmament remains key Israeli red line; ceasefire structurally fragile

active_escalation_events.event: RSF captures El Fasher — last major SAF stronghold in Darfur falls; mass killings, sexual violence, atrocities; UN fact-finding: 'hallmarks of genocide'
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Strategic inflection — RSF dominates all Darfur; SAF launches Kordofan counter-offensive

active_escalation_events.event: Chad border incident — RSF drone strikes kill 17 funeral-goers in Chad territory; President Déby orders retaliation and total border closure; risk of regional spillover
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Conflict risk of regional expansion into Chad; Sudan as trigger for Sahel destabilisation

active_escalation_events.event: UN Security Council sanctions RSF leaders including 'Butcher of El-Fasher'; resolution expresses grave concern over Kordofan
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Limited diplomatic response; no operational change; Quad mediation non-operational since late 2025

active_escalation_events.event: Min Aung Hlaing sworn as Myanmar president — legitimation gambit following Feb 4 junta-backed USDP election; condemned by UN and rights groups; major ethnic armed groups immediately reject junta's 100-day peace talks offer
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Junta attempting democratic veneer; resistance coalescence under SCEF may accelerate

active_escalation_events.event: SCEF (Steering Council for Emergence of Federal Democratic Union) alliance announced — unites KIA, KNU, CNF, KNPP, NUG, Three Brotherhood Alliance around federal democratic union; first meaningful unified command structure
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Potentially transformative — addresses historic fragmentation weakness of resistance; operational military authority granted to ethnic groups

active_escalation_events.event: DRC and M23/AFC sign interim peace monitoring mechanism in Switzerland; Apr 19 joint statement includes humanitarian access protocol and prisoner release within 10 days
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Most substantive step since Washington Accords Dec 2025; monitoring mechanism agreed; implementation unverified; South Kivu clashes continue

active_escalation_events.event: Kim Jong Un orders nuclear acceleration — rare political meeting orders officials to speed development of nuclear weapons and delivery systems including land and sea-launched missiles; Yongbyon complex expansion ongoing
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Formal leadership directive — accelerates already elevated nuclear posture

active_escalation_events.event: DPRK 7th missile test of 2026 — 4th in April; Hwasong-11 tactical ballistic missiles with cluster-bomb and fragmentation mine warheads; fired toward East Sea; South Korea/Japan/US detection confirmed
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Accelerating test tempo; cluster-bomb warhead innovation threatens South Korean military concentrations

active_escalation_events.event: PLA Justice Mission 2025 exercises (Dec 29-30) — largest Taiwan blockade simulation since 2022; zones entered Taiwan's territorial waters for first time; 27 rockets near Keelung; Type 075 amphibious assault ship deployed; no carriers — blockade/decapitation focus assessed
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Incremental escalation ladder — each exercise pushes closer; practice entering territorial waters first time

active_escalation_events.event: PLA Task Group 107 live-fire drills east of Philippines' Luzon Island — direct response to US-PH Balikatan 2026 (with Japan, Australia, Canada, France, New Zealand) near Taiwan Strait
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: China treating Balikatan as hostile rehearsal; counter-exercise near First Island Chain

active_escalation_events.event: India launches Operation Sindoor — targeted strikes on terrorist camps in Pakistan/PoK following Pahalgam massacre (26 civilians killed Apr 22 2025); 100+ terrorists, 9 camps eliminated; Pakistan responds with drones May 7-10; Pakistan DGMO calls Indian DGMO for ceasefire May 10
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Most serious India-Pakistan confrontation since Balakot 2019; established new Indian doctrine of forceful retaliation; nuclear signals not deterred India

frameworks.name: US-brokered bilateral talks (Witkoff/Kushner)
frameworks.status: Stalled — last trilateral Feb 16 2026; Russia 'not top priority' (Lavrov Apr 18 2026)
frameworks.key_obstacle: Russia demands Ukraine withdraw from Donbas; Ukraine proposes front-line freeze only

frameworks.name: Orthodox Easter ceasefire
frameworks.status: Collapsed — mutual accusations of thousands of violations

mediation_deescalation_architecture.un_security_council: Deadlocked — Russian veto; UN not operative mediation channel

frameworks.name: Oman mediation (indirect US-Iran talks)
frameworks.status: Active but impassed — talks in Muscat Apr 20 2026; 'totally an impasse' per Iranian official Apr 27
frameworks.key_obstacle: US demands nuclear program on table; Iran wants nuclear issue deferred until Hormuz reopened; Iranian leadership divided
frameworks.mediator: Oman (FM Al-Busaidi); Pakistan (intermediary for Iranian proposal)

frameworks.name: Iran proposal via Pakistan
frameworks.status: Received; US described as 'not good enough'; White House Situation Room meeting Apr 27
frameworks.terms_offered: Iran: reopen Hormuz + end war + defer nuclear; US: lift blockade + unfreeze assets + pause nuclear talks

frameworks.name: US-brokered ceasefire (Apr 16 2026)
frameworks.status: Nominally extended to mid-May; operationally unraveling — Netanyahu ordered 'vigorous attacks' Apr 26; 500+ Israeli violations per Hezbollah
frameworks.mediator: US (Trump); Trump plans to host Netanyahu + Lebanese President Aoun in Washington

frameworks.name: Trump Comprehensive Plan / Board of Peace (BoP)
frameworks.status: Phase 1 active — fragile; Phase 2 stalled on disarmament
frameworks.key_obstacle: Hamas rejected disarmament clause Apr 14 2026; 984 Palestinians killed since ceasefire Oct 2025
frameworks.ceasefire_status: Low-intensity continued — daily violations; Yellow Line boundary

frameworks.name: Quad mediation (US + UAE + Saudi Arabia + Egypt)
frameworks.status: Non-operational since Sep 2025 — diplomatic bandwidth shifted to Iran war

frameworks.name: UN Security Council
frameworks.status: Feb 26 2026: sanctions on RSF leaders; statement on Kordofan; no ceasefire mechanism

frameworks.name: Washington Accords (Dec 2025)
frameworks.status: In parliament; MONUSCO oversight not operational
frameworks.latest: Switzerland talks Apr 16-19 2026 — interim peace monitoring mechanism signed; humanitarian access protocol agreed

frameworks.name: Doha Process
frameworks.status: Superseded by Washington Accords; prior ceasefire repeatedly violated

alliance_security_architectures.activation_status: Article 5 not triggered; heightened deterrence posture
posture_changes_recent: Eastern Sentry launched Sep 2025 — enhanced vigilance after Russian drones violated airspace of Estonia, Finland, Latvia, Lithuania, Norway, Poland, Romania
Baltic Sentry (Jan 2025) — critical undersea infrastructure protection Baltic Sea
Germany-led brigade in Lithuania inaugurated May 2025; scaling to 5,000 troops by 2027
Defense spending: majority of members now meeting or exceeding 2% GDP; Romania 2.7% 2026; Poland >3%
New Force Model: hundreds of thousands at varying readiness; rapid deployment within days
Balikatan 2026 (US-PH with Japan, Australia, Canada, France, New Zealand): Apr 20-May 8 near Taiwan Strait

alliance_security_architectures.activation_status: Active — Pillar I (submarine) and Pillar II (advanced capabilities) progressing
posture_changes_recent: Pillar I: Virginia-class submarines to Australia from ~2032; AUKUS SSN on rotation in Western Australia ongoing
Pillar II: AI, quantum, cyber, hypersonic, electronic warfare cooperation progressing

alliance_security_architectures.activation_status: Active diplomatic coordination; no mutual defence clause
posture_changes_recent: Balikatan 2026 includes Japan and Australia (QUAD alignment)
India: balancing QUAD participation with Russia-India relationship; India abstains on UN Ukraine votes

alliance_security_architectures.activation_status: Functional diplomatic forum; no collective defence action; China-India divergence limits effectiveness
posture_changes_recent: Iran formally joined; Russia-China coordination deepening
India-Pakistan and India-China tensions constrain collective coherence

alliance_security_architectures.activation_status: Degraded — Armenia suspended participation after CSTO failed to respond to Azerbaijani offensive 2023
posture_changes_recent: Armenia de facto exit from active participation
Russian focus on Ukraine limits CSTO rapid reaction capability

alliance_security_architectures.activation_status: Active — Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in force; troops deployed; munitions flowing
posture_changes_recent: ~11,000 DPRK troops in Kursk early 2026; artillery units active Jan 2026
DPRK instructors returning from Ukraine with 21st-century warfare skills — structural capability transfer

conflict_baselines.notes: All indicators at structural wartime norm. Spring-Summer 2026 offensive under way (ISW: commenced Mar 17). Ukraine drone advantage degrading Russian advance tempo. Easter ceasefire collapsed Apr 11-12. Peace talks stalled — Russia 'not top priority' (Lavrov Apr 18).

version_history.change: Initial baseline seeding from Week 1 scoring
Notes updated with Apr 2026 events; indicator levels confirmed

indicatorBaselines.I2: FPV and Shahed operations are BASELINE — do not score above I2 Level 2 for routine drone exchange. Escalation signals: range extension, swarm doctrine, third-party cross-border incursion.

conflict_baselines.doctrine_note: Russia Nov 2024 nuclear doctrine: formally lowered threshold for nuclear use in response to conventional attacks supported by nuclear states. This is a PERSISTENT BASELINE SHIFT for I3 — not a weekly event. Subsequent Medvedev/MFA rhetoric is episodic (flag with episodic:true, do not increment I3).
conflict_baselines.recalc_note: Structural break override needed — Nov 2024 doctrine shift. Recalibrate I3 baseline from Nov 2024.

conflict_baselines.notes: Ceasefire holds at low friction but structurally fragile. Hamas rejected disarmament Apr 14 2026. 984 Palestinians killed since Oct 2025 ceasefire. IDF daily violations. Phase 2 stalled. F4 flag on I5 — ceasefire claims unverified; Hamas disarmament not proceeding.

version_history.change: Initial baseline seeding from Week 1 scoring
Notes updated with Hamas disarmament rejection and ongoing violations

indicatorBaselines.I2: Drone use is baseline — escalation signal is swarm doctrine or cross-border incursion only.

conflict_baselines.notes: Year 4. RSF captured El Fasher Oct 2025. Kordofan new flashpoint. SAF recaptured Kadugli Feb 2026. Chad border incident Mar 2026 (risk of spillover). 14M displaced. 33.7M need humanitarian aid. Famine confirmed North Darfur + South Kordofan. Quad mediation non-operational. I6 displacement continues at world's worst level.

version_history.change: Initial baseline seeding from Week 1 scoring
Notes updated with El Fasher fall, Kordofan dynamics, Chad incident

indicatorBaselines.I2: RSF drone use is structural shift from prior baseline — score as I2 Level 3 if confirmed given prior baseline.

conflict_baselines.recalc_note: Standard 3-year cycle (opened Apr 2023). Review all 6 indicators.

conflict_baselines.notes: Year 5+. Junta controls ~21% territory. Min Aung Hlaing sworn president Apr 10. SCEF resistance alliance formed Apr 15 — potentially transformative. Junta peace talks offer rejected by KNU and CNF. Iranian jet fuel confirmed sustaining air campaign. Junta weaker than at any point since 2021.

version_history.change: Initial baseline seeding from Week 1 scoring
Notes updated with Min Aung Hlaing presidency and SCEF formation

conflict_baselines.recalc_note: Structural break: 2024 territorial fragmentation. Recalibrate I1/I2 baseline.

conflict_baselines.notes: MSS→GSF transition creating security gap. Gangs controlling maritime/overland routes. GSF (Haitian-led security force) replacing MSS — transition period is high-risk. Gang control ~85% Port-au-Prince.

conflict_baselines.notes: F4 on I5. Washington Accords in parliament but MONUSCO oversight not operational. Switzerland talks Apr 16-19 produced interim peace monitoring mechanism and humanitarian access protocol. South Kivu fighting continues. M23 drone strikes Kisangani. Rubaya coltan mine (M23/Rwanda control) — strategic minerals link to US-DRC partnership.

conflict_baselines.notes: All at latent-conflict structural baseline. Justice Mission 2025 (Dec 29-30) largest exercise since 2022 — entered Taiwan's territorial waters. Two large-scale exercises in 2025 (Strait Thunder-2025A Apr; Justice Mission Dec). PLA counter-drills near Philippines Apr 25-26 responding to Balikatan 2026. PRC lesson-drawing from Iran-Israel conflict for I3-adjacent monitoring.

Notes updated with Justice Mission 2025 and Balikatan 2026 counter-drills

conflict_baselines.notes: I1/I2 above initial baseline. I3 at structural high baseline — not new deviation. 7th missile test 2026 by Apr 21; Kim ordered nuclear acceleration Feb 2026; ~11,000 troops in Russia/Ukraine. US redeploying Korea assets to Middle East creates window of reduced deterrence. DPRK seeking nuclear state recognition.

Notes updated with Kim nuclear acceleration order, missile tests, US asset redeployment

Operation Eternal Darkness Apr 8 (357 killed). Ceasefire Apr 16 extended to mid-May but nominally only — Netanyahu ordered vigorous attacks Apr 26; new evacuation orders Apr 27.

conflict_baselines.conflict: US–Israel vs Iran (Op Epic Fury / Roaring Lion)
conflict_baselines.notes: Three indicators at RED-band deviation levels. I3: 400kg enriched uranium at unknown location. I4: Hormuz closure — ~6 vessels/day vs 135 normally; Brent >$120; QatarEnergy force majeure. I5 elevated: Oman talks Apr 20 'consultations'; Iran proposal Apr 27 — US described as 'not good enough'; official: 'totally an impasse'. F3 on CENTCOM 'dismantled' claim. F4 on Iran decouple-Hormuz-from-nuclear proposal.

conflict_baselines.theatre: US-Israel vs Iran (Op Epic Fury / Roaring Lion)

f_flag_history.detail: Kremlin negotiating signals T3 only. No T2 corroboration. Lavrov Apr 18 confirmed Russia not prioritising talks.
Ceasefire/disarmament progress claims from Israeli/US officials (T3). Hamas disarmament rejected Apr 14 2026. Phase 2 stalled.
Doha/Washington/Angola ceasefire claims unverified operationally. Switzerland Apr 2026 talks produced protocol but MONUSCO oversight not functional.
Mutual drone attribution without forensic corroboration. Yale Humanitarian Lab Apr 2026: satellite imagery confirms Ethiopia-RSF supply route. Pattern consistent with false-flag seeding.
'Limited and targeted' Israeli framing inconsistent with operational scale (second division, Litani buffer zone, 700+ sorties, Op Eternal Darkness 357 killed).
CENTCOM claim of Iranian capabilities 'totally dismantled' contradicted by ongoing operations, Hormuz closure, Gulf attacks. Confirmed F3 Capability Theatre.

Iran Apr 27 proposal to decouple Hormuz from nuclear — conveyed via Pakistan. Iranian FM denied direct negotiations same day. US described offer as 'not good enough'. Official: 'totally an impasse'. Leadership division confirmed.

i5_calibration_2026.us_channel_note: US withdrawal from 66+ multilateral institutions means US participation in multilateral forums is no longer a reliable I5 positive indicator for 2026. Weight EU/Gulf/African Union mediation channels more heavily in Gaza, Ukraine, Sudan theatres.

IISS Military Balance 2026 precise OOB figures — this pass used ISW/news sources; formal IISS data for standing forces, interceptor stocks, naval tonnage not retrieved. Recommend IISS fetch for next refresh.

BWC/CWC compliance posture — listed in treaty spec scope; not fully populated this pass. Russia CWC use in Ukraine documented (Novichok Salisbury 2018; alleged use in Ukraine reported). Recommend dedicated fetch.
